name: Filet of Filly
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the filets of horse exactly as you would prepare filet mignon.
2. Wrap each filet with an uncooked bacon slice on the outside, then secure it with toothpicks.
3. Broil the bacon-wrapped filets until they reach your desired level of doneness.
Horse meat shares no parasites with humans, unlike pork and beef. The horse meat is fine-grained, tender, and lacks fat, offering a superb flavor that would be indistinguishable from beef if not specified otherwise. In my experience at the University of Arizona, I purchased whole horse filets at a reasonable price, which were US Grade A inspected. However, in this country, there seems to be an aversion to consuming horse meat. It is worth noting that in some states it is illegal to sell horsemeat for human consumption, which can be quite perplexing.
John Hartman
Indianapolis, IN
